expectations.evidence: Thin-client / thin-controller patterns are general software architecture knowledge. Naive Claude would likely catch direct DB access in services as a violation of standard patterns. This does not require Conduction-specific knowledge.

OpenRegister's typed event system (ObjectCreatingEvent, ObjectCreatedEvent, etc.) and the event-dispatch pattern in mappers are Conduction-specific. Naive Claude has no knowledge of this integration pattern.

expectations.text: Checks Vue Router usage (not custom hash routing)
expectations.evidence: Conduction's requirement for Vue Router history mode (not hash) is a convention in their apps but not a universal standard. Naive Claude would not know to check for this specific Conduction convention.

Reporting deviations from patterns is general code review behavior. Naive Claude would report any architectural issues it finds. However, Conduction-specific deviations (event dispatch missing, wrong store pattern) would be missed.

notes: General pattern violations (thin-client, deviation reporting) are detected by naive Claude. Conduction-specific patterns (OpenRegister event integration, Vue Router convention) are not.
